faqs.question: What causes drain flies in Lindenhurst homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies in Lindenhurst are typically caused by organic buildup in drains, pipes, and sewer lines. Long Island's humid climate and the area's older plumbing systems can accelerate the accumulation of the sludge and bacteria that drain fly larvae feed on. Bathrooms, basements, and commercial kitchens are the most common breeding sites.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Lindenhurst?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Lindenhurst take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of breeding sites. Our technicians will inspect all drains, apply bio-enzymatic cleaners and appropriate treatments, and provide prevention recommendations. You should see significant reduction within 2-3 days, with complete elimination in 1-2 weeks.

faqs.question: Is the drain fly treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, our drain fly treatments are completely safe for families and pets. We use EPA-approved products and bio-enzymatic solutions that break down organic matter without harsh chemicals. Most treatments involve targeted application directly into drains, minimizing exposure. You can remain in your home during and after treatment with no wait time required.
